post.description: API to create login token using email and password.Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call.
API to get login token using email and refresh token. Using valid refresh token, user can generate access token again without entering password. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call.

get.summary: Get car device linked data
get.description: API to get data of all the cars linked with devices for a single customer. You do not need to pass customer id, as this api will automatically detect from token which customer is requesting it. Return empty array if there are no data for car device linking. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

post.summary: Link single car device linked data by id
post.description: API to link single cars linked with single device for a customer. You do not need to pass customer id, as this api will automatically detect from token which customer is requesting it. Return error if there is any issue while linking. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

plate_id.description: The plate id number of the car

get.summary: Get Contacts
get.description: API to get contacts data. These contacts are just suggestions for the user while updated the information in Logbook for a particular route. When user enter a contact, it will be stored in the db and then will be shown to the user next time as a suggestion while entering the information. Contacts are shown only for that user who is making a request. Return empty array if there are no contacts. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

put.description: API to update Contact by id. These contacts are just suggestions for the user while updated the information in Logbook for a particular route. When user enter a contact, it will be stored in the db and then will be shown to the user next time as a suggestion while entering the information. In a contact a user can add the business partner, the company name to whom the user belong and the address as well. If user do not have permission then error will be shown. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Click Schema for parameters type

business_partner.description: The name of the business partner

distance_to_detect.description: The distance to detect in meters

delete.description: API to delete contact by id. It will delete the contact and the contact then will not be shown in suggestion the next time user is entering the information in a logbook for its route. If the contact does not exists, it will return an error. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Only admin can perform this operation

post.description: API to create a contact. API to update Contact by id. These contacts are just suggestions for the user while updated the information in Logbook for a particular route. When user enter a contact, it will be stored in the db and then will be shown to the user next time as a suggestion while entering the information. In a contact a user can add the business partner, the company name to whom the user belong and the address as well. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Click Schema for parameters type. Only admin can perform this operation

business_partner.description: The name of the business partner

distance_to_detect.description: The distance to detect in meters

get.summary: Get Customer Data
get.description: API to get customer data. If its admin then return all the customers and else only the logged in customer. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

get.summary: Get Customer Data by ID
get.description: API to get customer info by id. Admin can get any customer and if not admin then only get info of itself. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call and enter required parameters

put.description: API to update customer by ID. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Click Schema for parameters type

post.description: Api to get device data when it was on motion or iddle, click on 'Try it out' button to execute the call.

403.description: No permissions to access this device data

403.description: No permissions to access this device data

post.description: Api to get a single device data along with the total distance travelled in kilimeters(ki) or miles(mi) based on the date selection. User can enter start date, end date, distance unit (km, mi) and device id. If user enter device id which is not correct and it will show error. Please note that the distance calculation is based on point to point data pinged from device and it can very from other sources like goolge maps, goolge earth, valhalla etc. Click on 'Try it out' button to execute the call.

parameters.name: distanceUnit
parameters.description: km for kilometers and mi for miles

403.description: No permissions to access this device data

example.error: Device does not exists or you do not have permission

get.summary: Get Device Data
get.description: API to get device data. Only data will be shown for those devices who user have permissions. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

get.summary: Get Device Data by ID
get.description: API to get device data by id. If user dont have persmission of the ID then error will be shown. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call and enter the required parameters

get.summary: Get Drivers details
get.description: API to get driver data. These driver details are just suggestions for the user while updated the information in Logbook for a particular route. When user enter driver details, it will be stored in the db and then will be shown to the user next time as a suggestion while entering the information. In driver details for now, a user can add the first name and last name of the driver. Driver details are shown only for that user who is making a request. Return empty array if there are no drivers. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

post.description: API to create a driver. These driver details are just suggestions for the user while updated the information in Logbook for a particular route. When user enter driver details, it will be stored in the db and then will be shown to the user next time as a suggestion while entering the information. In driver details for now, a user can add the first name and last name of the driver. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Click Schema for parameters type. Only admin can perform this operation

first_name.description: The first name of the driver

last_name.description: The last name of the driver

put.description: API to update Driver by id. These driver details are just suggestions for the user while updated the information in Logbook for a particular route. When user enter driver details, it will be stored in the db and then will be shown to the user next time as a suggestion while entering the information. In driver details for now, a user can update the first name and last name of the driver. If user do not have permission then error will be shown. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Click Schema for parameters type

first_name.description: The first name of the driver

last_name.description: The last name of the driver

delete.description: API to delete driver by id. This will delete the drive details for a route in a logbook. After deleting the driver details will not be shown as a suggestion while entering information for a route in a logbook. If the driver does not exists, it will return an error. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Only admin can perform this operation

post.summary: Create Geofence of a specific device
post.description: API to create geofence for the provided device id. If the device is not permitted to the user then error will be shown. Enter the device id, shape type (1 for cirlce, add radius: 100 and center: [lng, lat] in options, 2 for rectanlge and 3 for polygon), options (to add colors) and coordinates where you want to draw a polygon. For circle you have to provide radius in options field as well. User can create multiple geofences for a single device. These geofences are not currently available and incompatible with v1. These will be available in v2. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Click Schema for parameters type

parameters.name: DeviceID
parameters.description: The id of the device for geofence

shape_type.description: The shape type: 1 for cirlce, 2 for rectanlge and 3 for polygon

options.description: The json string to defince colors and radius of the shape

post.summary: Get all geofences data for the given devices
post.description: API to get multiple geofences for multiple devices for the specific user. Enter all the device ids to get all geofences. If you enter wrong device id or device which you do not have permission then it will return no geofences for that device id. These geofences are not currently available and incompatible with v1. These will be available in v2. Please remove double quotations from the array before executing. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

deviceIDs.description: Array of different device ids to get the geofences

shape_type.description: The shape type: 1 for circle, 2 for rectangle and 3 for polygon

put.summary: Update specific geofence for a device
put.description: API to update geofence for the provided device id. If the device is not permitted to the user then error will be shown. Enter the device id, shape type (1 for cirlce, add radius: 100 and center: [lng, lat] in options, 2 for rectanlge and 3 for polygon), options (to add colors) and coordinates to udpate the geofence. For circle you have to provide radius in options field as well. These geofences are not currently available and incompatible with v1. These will be available in v2. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Click Schema for parameters type

parameters.name: GeofenceID
parameters.description: The id of the geofence to update

shape_type.description: The shape type: 1 for cirlce, 2 for rectanlge and 3 for polygon

options.description: The json string to defince colors and radius of the shape

shape_type.description: The shape type: 1 for circle, 2 for rectangle and 3 for polygon

delete.summary: Delete specific geofence for a device
delete.description: API to delete geofence for the provided device id. If the device is not permitted to the user then error will be shown. These geofences are not currently available and incompatible with v1. These will be available in v2. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Click Schema for parameters type

parameters.name: GeofenceID
parameters.description: The id of the geofence to delete

get.summary: Get Notifications of all devices of a customer
get.description: API to get data of notifications of all devices for a single customer. You do not need to pass customer id, as this api will automatically detect from token which customer is requesting it. User can pass alert type to get specific notifications and also read flag to 0 and 1 to get read and unread notifications. Alert types ranges from 1 to 22 which are: 1 -> Shock Alert, 2 -> Battery Alert, 3 -> Radius Alert, 4 -> SOS Alert, 5 -> Speed Alert, 6 -> Power Cut-off Alert, 7 -> Ignition Alert, 9 -> Drop Alert, 10 -> Area Enter Alert, 11 -> Area Leave Alert, 13 -> Voltage Alert, 22 -> Turn off Alert. 0 -> unread and 1 -> read notificaitons. Return empty array if there are no notifications. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

parameters.name: isRead
parameters.description: Get read and unread notifications. 0 for unread and 1 for read

get.summary: Get Notifications of a single devices of a customer
get.description: API to get data of notifications of a single devices for a customer. You do not need to pass customer id, as this api will automatically detect from token which customer is requesting it. User can pass alert type to get specific notifications and also read flag to 0 and 1 to get read and unread notifications. Alert types ranges from 1 to 22 which are: 1 -> Shock Alert, 2 -> Battery Alert, 3 -> Radius Alert, 4 -> SOS Alert, 5 -> Speed Alert, 6 -> Power Cut-off Alert, 7 -> Ignition Alert, 9 -> Drop Alert, 10 -> Area Enter Alert, 11 -> Area Leave Alert, 13 -> Voltage Alert, 22 -> Turn off Alert. 0 -> unread and 1 -> read notificaitons. Return empty array if there are no notifications. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

parameters.name: isRead
parameters.description: Get read and unread notifications. 0 for unread and 1 for read

get.summary: Get Notifications of a single devices of a customer using custom date
get.description: API to get data of notifications of a single devices for a customer. You do not need to pass customer id, as this api will automatically detect from token which customer is requesting it. User can pass alert type to get specific notifications and also read flag to 0 and 1 to get read and unread notifications. Alert types ranges from 1 to 22 which are: 1 -> Shock Alert, 2 -> Battery Alert, 3 -> Radius Alert, 4 -> SOS Alert, 5 -> Speed Alert, 6 -> Power Cut-off Alert, 7 -> Ignition Alert, 9 -> Drop Alert, 10 -> Area Enter Alert, 11 -> Area Leave Alert, 13 -> Voltage Alert, 22 -> Turn off Alert. 0 -> unread and 1 -> read notificaitons. Return empty array if there are no notifications. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

parameters.name: alertType
parameters.description: Alert type range from 1 to 22. If not passed then it will return all types of notifications.

parameters.name: noOfNotification
parameters.description: Number of notifications to return, if not passed then it will return last 50 notifications.

parameters.name: isRead
parameters.description: Get read and unread notifications. 0 for unread and 1 for read. . If not passed then it will return all notifications.

put.description: API to mark notifications as read or unread of a single devices for a customer. You do not need to pass customer id, as this api will automatically detect from token which customer is requesting it. User can pass alert type for specific notifications and also read flag to 0 and 1 to update read and unread notifications. Alert types ranges from 1 to 22 which are: 1 -> Shock Alert, 2 -> Battery Alert, 3 -> Radius Alert, 4 -> SOS Alert, 5 -> Speed Alert, 6 -> Power Cut-off Alert, 7 -> Ignition Alert, 9 -> Drop Alert, 10 -> Area Enter Alert, 11 -> Area Leave Alert, 13 -> Voltage Alert, 22 -> Turn off Alert. 0 -> unread and 1 -> read notificaitons. Return count for updated and zero count if there are no updates in notifications. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

parameters.name: isRead
parameters.description: Update read and unread notifications. 0 for unread and 1 for read

put.description: API to mark notifications as read or unread of all devices for a single customer requesting it. You do not need to pass customer id, as this api will automatically detect from token which customer is requesting it. User can pass alert type for specific notifications and also read flag to 0 and 1 to update read and unread notifications. Alert types ranges from 1 to 22 which are: 1 -> Shock Alert, 2 -> Battery Alert, 3 -> Radius Alert, 4 -> SOS Alert, 5 -> Speed Alert, 6 -> Power Cut-off Alert, 7 -> Ignition Alert, 9 -> Drop Alert, 10 -> Area Enter Alert, 11 -> Area Leave Alert, 13 -> Voltage Alert, 22 -> Turn off Alert. 0 -> unread and 1 -> read notificaitons. Return success message if the api updates successfully. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

parameters.name: isRead
parameters.description: Update read and unread notifications. 0 for unread and 1 for read

get.summary: Get Reasons
get.description: API to get reasons. This reason means why you took that particular route and it will be stored in the db along with its route for your logbook. So, the next time you see a route for the logbook, you can see the reason and understand why you took that route. These reasons are just suggestions for the user while entering the information in Logbook for a particular route. When user enters the reason, it will be stored in the db and then will be shown to the user next time as a suggestion while entering the information. Reasons are shown only for that user who is making a request. Return empty array if there are no drivers. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

post.description: API to create a Reason. This reason means why you took that particular route and it will be stored in the db along with its route for your logbook. So, the next time you see a route for the logbook, you can see the reason and understand why you took that route. These reasons are just suggestions for the user while entering the information in Logbook for a particular route. When user enters the reason, it will be stored in the db and then will be shown to the user next time as a suggestion while entering the information. Reasons are shown only for that user who is making a request. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Click Schema for parameters type. Only admin can perform this operation

put.description: API to update reason by id. This reason means why you took that particular route and it will be stored in the db along with its route for your logbook. So, the next time you see a route for the logbook, you can see the reason and understand why you took that route. These reasons are just suggestions for the user while entering the information in Logbook for a particular route. When user enters the reason, it will be stored in the db and then will be shown to the user next time as a suggestion while entering the information. Reasons are shown only for that user who is making a request. If user do not have permission then error will be shown. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Click Schema for parameters type

delete.description: API to delete Reason by id. This will delete the reason from the db and will not be shown the next time you are entering the information for a route in your logbook. If the reason does not exists, it will return an error. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call. Only admin can perform this operation

get.summary: Generate logbook routes of a single device
get.description: API to generate routes of a single device. In the api just pass device id to generate routes. If device is linked with a car then it will create routes under the logbook otherewise normal route. Upon sucess user will receive a success message. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

get.summary: Generate addresses of logbook routes of a single device
get.description: API to generate addresses of a single device. In the api just pass device id and addresses of routes which are not generated will be generated. Upon sucess user will receive a success message. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

get.summary: Get all routes of a device
get.description: API to get routes of a single device. In the api all the routes of a single device will be shown present in the logbook. The route contains the starting and ending coordinates, the distance coverted by the device and other details like the type of route and driver, company details. Routes are shown only for that user who is making a request. Return empty array if there are no routes or show error for invalid device id. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

iddevice.description: the db id from get device get api

startID.description: the start time in unix timestamp

endID.description: the end time in unix timestamp

optimized_distance.description: the distance after snapping to road

route_type.description: the route type, 1 for private, 2 for towork, 3 for business and 4 for mix

contact.description: the id of the business contact attached with the route

reason.description: the id of the reason attached with the route

driver.description: the id of the driver attached with the route

start_address.description: the start address of the lat, lng of the route

end_address.description: the end address of the lat, lng of the route

carDevice_id.description: the id of the logbook attached with the device

mileage.description: mileage being calculated using point to point

optimized_mileage.description: mileage being calculated using snap to road

get.summary: Get all routes of a device along with the linked car
get.description: API to get routes of a single device which is linked with a signle car. In the api all the routes of a single device which is linked with a single car will be shown present in the logbook. The route contains the starting and ending coordinates, the distance coverted by the device and other details like the type of route and driver, company details. Routes are shown only for that user who is making a request. Return empty array if there are no routes or show error for invalid device id. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

parameters.name: CarDevice_id
parameters.description: The ID of the carDevice linked in the car_device table

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page no. to get more routes

iddevice.description: the db id from get device get api

startID.description: the start time in unix timestamp

endID.description: the end time in unix timestamp

optimized_distance.description: the distance after snapping to road

route_type.description: the route type, 1 for private, 2 for towork, 3 for business and 4 for mix

contact.description: the id of the business contact attached with the route

reason.description: the id of the reason attached with the route

driver.description: the id of the driver attached with the route

start_address.description: the start address of the lat, lng of the route

end_address.description: the end address of the lat, lng of the route

carDevice_id.description: the id of the logbook attached with the device

mileage.description: mileage being calculated using point to point

optimized_mileage.description: mileage being calculated using snap to road

post.summary: Get all last positions for the given devices
post.description: API to get all last positions of the given devices for the specific user. Enter all the device ids to get all devices that you want to see the last positions. You can also pass 'dateunix' for each device and pass parameter 'fromLastPoint', then it will get all the points starting from the dateunix to the current time. If you enter wrong device id or device which you do not have permission then it will return no last position for that device id. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

deviceIDs.description: Array of different device ids to get the last positions

get.summary: Get Device last Sensor data
get.description: API to get last voltage data of the specific device. Click on the 'Try it out' button to execute the call

did.description: the db id from get device endpoint
